normalization.notes: Store Khmer text in NFC while preserving source order for dependent vowels, coeng signs, subscripts, and diacritics. Analysis tooling may inspect orthographic syllables and visible clusters without rewriting source text.

segmentation.pedagogical_units: consonant bases, inherent-vowel series, dependent vowel signs by visual position, coeng subscripts, final consonants, diacritics, and punctuation
segmentation.notes: Khmer is written mostly without spaces between words. Count words, orthographic syllables, visible clusters, and teachable base-plus-vowel units separately because vowels can appear before, above, below, after, or around the consonant.
